mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand growth that lifts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive mean return over 60 days in more than 60 percent of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during post-shock recovery phases indicate capacity utilization improvements that boost manufacturing margins and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of observations during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that directly increases corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220, accelerating inflation forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 220, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with declining real personal consumption expenditures indicates a decoupling of job growth from consumer spending that signals economic fragility and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment, any upward movement in the federal funds rate triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward fixed income, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of rate hike periods within the low-rate regime, or the signal loses directional accuracy when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent.
